notes: Cow's milk is one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Watch for hidden milk in baked goods, processed meats, chocolates.
Eggs are one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Found in baked goods, pasta, mayonnaise, some wines.
Peanuts (groundnuts) are one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Even trace amounts can cause anaphylaxis.
Tree nuts are one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Each nut species has distinct allergenic proteins.
Soy is one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Very widely used in processed foods.
Wheat is one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Distinct from celiac/gluten sensitivity.
Gluten is a protein in wheat, barley, rye. Celiac patients must strictly avoid. EU labelling requirement.
Fish is one of the 9 major FDA allergens. Different fish species have distinct allergenic proteins.
Shellfish (crustaceans and molluscs) is one of the 9 major FDA allergens.
Sesame became the 9th major FDA allergen in 2023. Found in tahini, hummus, bread toppings.
Mustard is one of the EU's 14 listed allergens. Common in dressings, sauces, processed meats.
Celery is one of the EU's 14 listed allergens. Found in stocks, soups, processed foods.
Sulphites must be declared if >10ppm. Common in wine, dried fruits, processed foods. Can trigger asthma.
Lupin is one of the EU's 14 listed allergens. Found in some bread and pasta as an alternative flour.
